passage.title: Weather Station Update
passage.text: The weather station team wanted children to read simple science directions more clearly. At first, the team made a direction wall that mixed several steps in one box, but children missed the next move because each box held too much text. The plant club said "The board is bright, yet I still forget what to do next." After hearing that, the team made the start point and finish point much larger and used the same arrow style across the whole board. They also placed one extra guide at the measuring table so the extra guide repeated the next action before mistakes happened. On the next test day, children followed the science path in a steadier order during the new trial. The team noticed that short action words helped more than long science notes. Because of that result, the class chose to use the new clear format for future experiments too.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Making science steps easier for children to understand
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Each box held too much text
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group set beside each table?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
A mini reminder sign
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does icon mean in the fourth sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
A small picture that stands for something
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: Shorter clues reduced confusion during the experiment
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Apply the new format to future science activities
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
